skill.name: specdd-docs
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to write or update documentation from active SpecDD specs.
skill.summary: Generate and update project documentation from SpecDD specification files.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: All 10 static analysis findings are false positives. The 9 external_commands alerts were triggered by Markdown inline code backticks (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`), not shell execution. The high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding was caused by the Apache-2.0 license identifier text, not actual cryptography. This skill is a documentation generation helper with no external commands, network access, or file system writes beyond standard AI tool output. No prompt injection detected. Safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 9 instances of backtick characters as Ruby shell execution. All backticks are Markdown inline code formatting for file paths and command names. No actual shell commands are present.

low_findings.title: License identifier misidentified as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Apache-2.0' on line 3 as a 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a standard SPDX license identifier, not cryptography.

content.value_statement: Writing documentation from specification files is time-consuming and easy to get wrong. This skill automatically generates accurate developer docs that stay aligned with your SpecDD specs, contracts, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Generate documentation from active SpecDD spec chains
Update existing docs when specs change
Resolve bootstrap files in the correct priority order
Identify governing specs before editing documentation
Report spec gaps found during documentation review
Preserve operational constraints from specs in user-facing docs
limitations: Requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Cannot infer write authority from filenames or conventions without spec definitions
Will not invent rules, APIs, or guarantees not supported by specs
Cannot adopt SpecDD in a non-SpecDD project (use specdd-adopt instead)

use_cases.title: Generate API docs from spec contracts
use_cases.description: A developer needs to create user-facing API documentation from existing SpecDD specification contracts. The skill resolves the spec chain and generates accurate reference docs.

use_cases.title: Update docs after spec changes
use_cases.description: A team updates specification contracts for a new feature release. The skill re-reads the changed specs and updates corresponding documentation to match.

use_cases.title: Audit documentation for spec alignment
use_cases.description: A lead developer reviews documentation for accuracy. The skill compares existing docs against active specs and reports gaps or outdated content.

prompt_templates.title: Generate docs from a single spec file
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the spec file at [spec_path] and generate matching documentation. Include all contracts, constraints, and behaviors defined in the spec.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs documentation for a single specification file

prompt_templates.title: Update docs after spec modification
prompt_templates.prompt: The spec at [spec_path] has been updated. Read the current spec and update the corresponding documentation file at [doc_path] to reflect the changes. Do not expand the contract.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer modified a spec and needs docs updated to match

prompt_templates.title: Generate full doc suite from spec chain
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the spec chain for the target at [target_path] using the active bootstrap. Generate documentation for each spec in the chain. Report any spec gaps found.
prompt_templates.scenario: A tech lead needs comprehensive docs for a complex module

prompt_templates.title: Cross-reference docs against multiple specs
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the existing documentation at [doc_path]. Resolve the full spec chain including ancestor specs and References. Verify every claim in the docs against a governing spec. List any claims not supported by specs.
prompt_templates.scenario: A reviewer needs to audit existing docs for spec compliance

output_examples.input: Read the spec file at specs/api/orders.md and generate user-facing documentation.
output: A documentation file covering all endpoints defined in the Orders spec, including request parameters, response formats, error codes, and operational constraints. Each section maps to the corresponding spec contract.
A summary of any spec gaps found during documentation review.

output_examples.input: The spec at specs/api/auth.md was updated with new rate limiting rules. Update the docs at docs/auth.md to reflect these changes.
output: The auth documentation was updated to include the new rate limiting section under a 'Throttling' heading, matching the operational constraints defined in the updated spec. No existing contracts were modified or expanded.
A change log entry documenting what was updated and which spec version governed the changes.

best_practices: Always resolve the full bootstrap chain before beginning documentation work to ensure correct write authority
Treat specs as source-adjacent contracts and documentation as explanatory material that must not expand the contract
Report spec gaps when documentation review reveals underspecified behavior instead of inventing rules
anti_patterns: Rereading the entire spec chain when a workflow phase changes - reread only the narrowest relevant spec
Inferring write authority from similar filenames or conventions without explicit spec definitions
Adding promises, APIs, or workflows to documentation that are not supported by specs or implementation

faq.question: What does SpecDD mean?
faq.answer: SpecDD is a specification-driven development methodology where contracts and specs are treated as source-adjacent artifacts that govern implementation and documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill work with non-SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file. Use the specdd-adopt skill to add SpecDD to a new project.

faq.question: Can this skill modify spec files?
faq.answer: No. This skill reads specs and writes documentation. It does not modify specification contracts.

faq.question: Does this skill use external CLI tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates entirely through file reading and writing. It does not execute shell commands.

faq.question: How does the skill handle conflicting specs?
faq.answer: The skill reads bootstrap files in a defined priority order and applies them sequentially. Later files narrow earlier rules but must not silently weaken project contracts.

faq.question: What happens when a spec is missing?
faq.answer: The skill reports the missing spec as a gap and stops. It will not invent rules or behaviors to fill the gap.
